insight: If your opponent accidentally skips an early turn, you can offer them the turn back by ending your turn without harvesting any drones. This gesture stops working reliably once you have units that automatically generate resources, such as Conduits or Tarsiers.
author: amalloy
context: amalloy explaining sportsmanship conventions around accidental turn skips to a newer player.

insight: Urban Sentry (cost 4G1B, 3 HP, start-of-turn gain 1 attack) functions as a strong first attacker and more accessible anti-threat absorber compared to Xeno Guardian, because it only requires a single blue tech building rather than double Blastforge. Going Sentry into Xeno Guardian is considered smoother than Wall into Xeno Guardian.
author: consensus
context: Community discussion immediately after Urban Sentry was revealed on stream, comparing it to Xeno Guardian and other absorbers.

insight: Urban Sentry is particularly strong against Tatsu Nullifier because it can absorb the chill damage while also contributing attack, allowing a blue-green player to handle the freeze threat without dedicating separate defensive units.
author: consensus
context: Community discussing Urban Sentry's defensive niches after its reveal, with bky and darkeisbein agreeing it is well-suited against Tatsu.

insight: A P1 opening of DD > A > DE (third Engineer) threatens to kill both P2 Engineers early and pressures P2 toward a Fastimus (fast Animus) response. This line likely denies or severely complicates the standard DDE opening for P2, since P2 risks losing both Engineers to the early Animus threat.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of opening theory in the context of the newly revealed Tyranno Smorcus, examining how early Animus pressure interacts with P2 lines.

insight: Tyranno Smorcus paired with Animus allows a player to alternate spending 6 gold (buy Smorcus) and 5RR (click Animus + Smorcus) turns, effectively producing 2 attack every 2 turns at roughly 13 gold total cost with no ongoing tech commitment after the Animus. This is comparable to Perforator efficiency when Perforators would otherwise be lost on defense, but unlike Perforators, Smorcus cannot block.
author: consensus
context: Expert players bky, mrguy888, extratricky, and darkeisbein debating the efficiency of Smorcus+Animus versus Perforator lines.

insight: With only 4 supply of Tyranno Smorcus, a granularity exploit exists: buying 11->1A->AA (delaying an Animus purchase until the last Smorcus turn) saves 1 gold versus an even 1A->1A->1A spread. The low supply cap limits how far into the future this borrowing extends, making it worth conserving the last Smorcus copy to buy on a turn where you would otherwise decline to click another Smorcus.
author: .bky_1556
context: bky analyzing granularity economics specific to Tyranno Smorcus's 4-unit supply cap.

insight: Xeno Guardian is considered by experienced players to be borderline overpowered: it is nearly unskippable in any set it appears in (unless a better absorber is also present), every Xeno set defaults to double-Blastforge, and it offers the best value-per-cost among 4-HP absorbers, far ahead of alternatives like Proto-Mech or Mahar Rectifier.
author: consensus
context: redrame, mtanzer, darkeisbein, and bky discussing Xeno Guardian's power level relative to Urban Sentry.

insight: Soaking on a Chieftain at lifespan 1 is generally more efficient than pseudo-absorbing (holding it back) at higher lifespans. However, blocking with a Chieftain on its first turn is frequently correct in wall-absorb sets. Soaking at lifespan 3 is considered underrated by some experts, though the more common decision points are lifespan 1 vs. lifespan 2.
author: consensus
context: Extended expert discussion between awaclus, mtanzer, darkeisbein, and bky on optimal Chieftain soak timing.

insight: Iso Kronus produces attack in large alternating spikes (like 4 attack every other turn) rather than steady output, which is very strong when synchronized with other even-turn attackers such as Scorch. However, opponents at higher levels learn to handle these large swings, reducing its effectiveness relative to its apparent power at lower skill levels.
author: .bky_1556
context: Discussion of unit strength across skill levels, with bky noting the learning curve for opponents facing Iso Kronus.

insight: Several advanced units (Wincer, Tia Threnody, Iso Kronus, Endotherm Kit) are rated significantly stronger at low ELO than high ELO because lower-skill opponents systematically over- or under-defend against irregular or complex attack patterns, making these units punishing even with suboptimal play.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of whether unit power rankings depend on player ELO, with multiple community members contributing examples.

insight: The newly revealed unit (referred to in context as having a charge-doubling mechanic) has strong synergy with Cryo Ray: it reduces the gauss-charges-per-damage ratio for Cryo Ray from roughly 2-3 charges per damage down to approximately 1 charge per damage, dramatically improving Cryo Ray's efficiency.
author: extratricky
context: extratricky and redzonex analyzing the new unit's synergies with existing charge-based units including Cryo Ray and Iso Kronus.
